/cwe/weakness/420.Name: Unprotected Alternate Channel
/cwe/weakness/420.Description: The product protects a primary channel, but it does not use the same level of protection for an alternate channel.

ModesOfIntroduction.Note: OMISSION: This weakness is caused by missing a security tactic during the architecture and design phase.

Entries.IntroText:
Register SECURE_ME is located at address 0xF00. A mirror of this register called COPY_OF_SECURE_ME is at location 0x800F00. The register SECURE_ME is protected from malicious agents and only allows access to select, while COPY_OF_SECURE_ME is not. 


 Access control is implemented using an allowlist (as indicated by acl_oh_allowlist). The identity of the initiator of the transaction is indicated by the one hot input, incoming_id. This is checked against the acl_oh_allowlist (which contains a list of initiators that are allowed to access the asset). 


 Though this example is shown in Verilog, it will apply to VHDL as well.

Entries.BodyText: The bugged line of code is repeated in the Bad example above. The weakness arises from the fact that the SECURE_ME register can be modified by writing to the shadow register COPY_OF_SECURE_ME. The address of COPY_OF_SECURE_ME should also be included in the check. That buggy line of code should instead be replaced as shown in the Good Code Snippet below.

PotentialMitigations.Description: Identify all alternate channels and use the same protection mechanisms that are used for the primary channels.
